title: Data Analyst - Marketing Intelligence
description_snippet:
When travelers are searching for a hotel, we want the obvious choice to be trivago! Our leading metasearch engine is super fast and constantly optimized - enabling millions of travelers to compare hotel prices from hundreds of booking sites and find great deals in just a few clicks. We use cutting-edge technology, real-time auction, and machine learning techniques with petabytes of data to create an experience - time and money saved! In the lively city of Düsseldorf, we seize opportunities to learn everyday, innovate, and make an enduring mark on the travel industry. At trivago you will find those who aren’t afraid of change but rather embrace it, turning every challenge into a pathway for growth. Join trivago, work with a great team, and grow with us!



Join us in making a difference
description_full:
When travelers are searching for a hotel, we want the obvious choice to be trivago! Our leading metasearch engine is super fast and constantly optimized - enabling millions of travelers to compare hotel prices from hundreds of booking sites and find great deals in just a few clicks. We use cutting-edge technology, real-time auction, and machine learning techniques with petabytes of data to create an experience - time and money saved! In the lively city of Düsseldorf, we seize opportunities to learn everyday, innovate, and make an enduring mark on the travel industry. At trivago you will find those who aren’t afraid of change but rather embrace it, turning every challenge into a pathway for growth. Join trivago, work with a great team, and grow with us!



Join us in making a difference



The Marketing Strategy and Intelligence team at trivago is a unique blend of data and business expertise, steering key marketing decisions through impactful data products, actionable insights, and deep market understanding. As a Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in shaping and executing trivago's global marketing strategy. Your work will empower teams to make informed choices that drive growth, efficiency, and innovation across all marketing channels.

You will collaborate closely with data scientists, analysts, engineers as well as growth managers and business stakeholders to transform complex data into real business value. Together, you will design advanced analytics solutions, develop insightful dashboards and reports, and uncover strategic opportunities through in-depth research, modeling and experimentation. By providing clear, actionable insights, you will help optimize campaigns, identify market trends, and guide the decision-making process.

If this sounds like you, read on & apply today!



How you’ll make an impact:


Translate data into actionable insights that shape our marketing strategy and investments.
Partner with marketing stakeholders to identify opportunities, define KPIs, and measure success.
Build clear, engaging dashboards and reports that highlight trends and tell compelling stories.
Conduct deep dives to understand performance drivers, uncover anomalies, and recommend actions.
Ensure marketing data quality and consistency across multiple sources.
Share your insights in a way that resonates with both technical and non-technical audiences.


What you'll need to thrive:


5+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, ideally in marketing, e-commerce, or digital growth.
A degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, or another quantitative field.
Strong business acumen - you connect the dots between numbers, customer behavior, and company goals.
Proficiency in SQL and hands-on experience with BI/dashboarding tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker).
Proven ability to tell stories with data - making complex findings clear, engaging, and actionable.
Solid understanding of marketing metrics, attribution, and campaign performance.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal.


Stand out with:


Experience in the travel, e-commerce, or digital marketing industry.
Hands-on experience with A/B testing, geo testing, or causal inference.
Knowledge of big data platforms like BigQuery.
Familiarity with Python or R for analysis.

Worried about missing a few requirements? Still apply, and express your motivation as you may just be the right candidate for this or other roles!



What you can look forward to:



As you grow, so do we.

This is why at trivago, we prioritize your development, offer personalized coaching through Nilo, and provide workshops, educational meetups, conferences, free online learning courses, and access to a fully-equipped campus library.

Moving to join us?

No problem. You can count on the visa support from our talent support team, a relocation package, interest-free newcomer loan, free language classes, regular team and company-wide events to build experiences together.

description_full:
Senior Marketing Analyst (m/w/d) - E-Commerce & Online Marketing




EPODEX | Vollzeit | Berichtet direkt an: Head of Marketing / Geschäftsführung




Wer wir sind

EPODEX wächst – und mit uns wächst der Anspruch an datengetriebene Entscheidungen. Wir glauben nicht daran, Bauchgefühl zur Strategie zu machen. Wir glauben daran, dass Daten Geschichten erzählen – wenn man weiß, wie man ihnen zuhört. Genau dafür suchen wir dich.




Deine Mission

Arbeite nicht einfach nur - gestalte gemeinsam mit uns.

Du bist nicht nur Analyst. Du bist der Taktgeber, der datenbasierte Klarheit in unsere Wachstumsstrategie bringt. Du erkennst, was andere übersehen, und verwandelst Zahlen in Entscheidungen, die EPODEX voranbringen. Du arbeitest direkt an der Schnittstelle zwischen Performance, Strategie und Unternehmensführung - sichtbar, wirkungsvoll und nah am Business.

 

Deine Aufgaben

Datenerhebung & Tracking - Die Grundlage für alles

Ohne saubere Daten keine belastbaren Entscheidungen. Du sorgst dafür, dass unser Datenfundament steht - und stabil bleibt.

Du überwachst unser Tracking-Setup ganzheitlich und stellst sicher, dass jede relevante Nutzerinteraktion korrekt und vollständig erfasst wird – über alle Kanäle hinweg.
Du definierst und pflegst eine konsistente Marketing-Parameter-Struktur (z. B. UTM-Taxonomien), die kanalübergreifend Vergleichbarkeit schafft und Auswertungen skalierbar macht.
Du begleitest aktiv die Tag-Manager-Konfiguration und arbeitest eng mit dem Data Engineering zusammen, um neue Tracking-Anforderungen schnell und sauber umzusetzen.
Du etablierst Prozesse zur kontinuierlichen Qualitätssicherung unserer Daten - du erkennst Abweichungen bevor sie zu Fehlentscheidungen führen.

 

Kampagnen-Analyse - Verstehen, was wirklich funktioniert

Du analysierst nicht um der Analyse willen - du analysierst, um zu handeln.

Du bewertest die Performance unserer Kampagnen auf allen relevanten Ebenen: Kanal, Kampagne, Anzeigengruppe, Creatives - und leitest daraus klare Optimierungsimpulse ab.
Du analysierst zentrale KPIs wie ROAS, CPM, CPC und CTR nicht isoliert, sondern im Kontext von Markt, Saison und Wettbewerb - und siehst das Gesamtbild.
Du empfiehlst datenbasiert, wie Budgets zwischen Kanälen und Kampagnen allokiert werden sollten, um maximale Effizienz und Reichweite zu erzielen.
Du erkennst Muster in der Saisonalität und beziehst externe Faktoren (Trends, Marktlage, Plattformveränderungen) aktiv in deine Einschätzungen ein.

 

Conversion & Funnel - Potenziale sehen, Hebel finden

Wachstum entsteht nicht immer durch mehr Traffic - sondern durch besseres Verständnis des Nutzers.

Du analysierst Conversion Rates differenziert nach Kanal, Gerät, Landingpage und Zielgruppe - und weißt, wo wir Nutzer verlieren und warum.
Du identifizierst Funnel-Drop-offs mit chirurgischer Präzision und arbeitest mit dem Team daran, sie systematisch zu schließen.
Du konzipierst und wertest A/B- sowie multivariate Tests sowohl für Paid- als auch für Owned-Channel-Formate aus - von der Hypothese bis zur Handlungsempfehlung.
Du bringst den Testing-Gedanken aktiv ins Unternehmen: Kein Bauchgefühl, kein Zufall - Lernen durch strukturierte Experimente.

 

Attribution - Gerechtigkeit für jeden Touchpoint

Du weißt, dass der letzte Klick selten der entscheidende ist.

Du baust ein belastbares Multi-Touch-Attributionsmodell auf, das abbildet, wie unsere Kanäle zusammenspielen - und wo der echte Wertbeitrag liegt.
Du definierst, pflegst und vergleichst verschiedene Attributionsmodelle und zeigst transparent auf, welche Entscheidungen sie beeinflussen.
Du erkennst Kanal-Kannibalisierung frühzeitig und entwickelst Maßnahmen, um Ressourcen zielgerichtet und ohne Reibungsverlust einzusetzen.
Du schaffst die Grundlage dafür, dass Budget-Entscheidungen nicht mehr auf Annahmen, sondern auf echtem Verständnis der Customer Journey basieren.




Was du mitbringst

Fachliche Qualifikationen

Mindestens 5 Jahre Berufserfahrung in der datengetriebenen Analyse vo

description_full:
As a Senior Marketing Analyst (all genders), you will be part of our newly established Business Insights team, which brings together Analytics and UX Research to enable well-informed decisions in both marketing and broader business contexts. You act as an analytical sparring partner for our Growth Marketing team, translating marketing and customer data into robust insights, identifying growth opportunities across the entire funnel, and supporting data-driven decision-making at both strategic and operational levels.

Always with the goal of creating measurable impact for users and the business, while ensuring and continuously evolving a reliable, technically sound tracking foundation.

You work closely with Marketing and Product and play a key role in ensuring that our marketing initiatives are measurable, scalable, and efficient.




Minimum Qualifications and skills

You have 5+ years of professional experience as a Marketing Analyst (all genders), Growth Analyst (all genders), BI Analyst (all genders), or in a comparable analytical role.
You bring a deep understanding of growth and performance marketing, including areas such as paid channels, CRM, and lifecycle marketing.
You work confidently with SQL (advanced proficiency) as well as common BI tools (e.g.Looker, Tableau, Power BI or similar).
You bring strong experience in web and app marketing tracking (e.g.Google Analytics, event tracking, attribution) and have the technical understandingrequiredtoactively further develop andoptimizetracking setups.
You have experience using Python and/or R for advanced analyses, modeling, or forecasting.
You havevery good English skills, both written and spoken.




Preferred Qualifications and skills

You are familiar with common marketing attribution models and their practical application.
You have ideally worked in a data-driven scale-up, digital, or tech environment.
You have good German language skills, both written and spoken.




What the role is about

You analyze marketing and growth initiatives across the entire customer funnel and assess their impact.
You develop and own core KPIs, dashboards, and reports for Growth and Marketing teams.
You conduct in-depthcohort,funnel,attribution, and ROI analyses.
You work closely with Growth Marketing, analyze campaigns, support experiments, and enable data-driven budget and channel decisions.
You derive clear, actionable recommendations for stakeholders at different levels of the organization.
You continuously improve data models, tracking, and attribution concepts while ensuring high data quality.
You design and support Geo-tests, A/B tests and experiments and evaluate their impact on growth and efficiency.
You communicate complex analyses clearly and tailor insights to different stakeholderaudiences.
You challenge assumptions, define meaningful success metrics, and select apropriate analytical methods.
You support the growth of the Business Insights Team through mentoring and knowledge sharing.

Constantly adding new perspectives is key to our success. We will never judge anyone by age, sex, family background, color of skin, religious beliefs nor sexual orientation. description_full:
Job Purpose

As Commercial Optimisation Lead / Incentives Analyst, you will be responsible for ensuring that incentives, rewards, and promotional spend are commercially effective, sustainable, and well controlled. The role focuses on analysing promotional performance, supporting data-driven offer design, and ensuring that incentive strategies maximise commercial impact while managing cost exposure and risk.

 

 Responsabilities: 

Analyse the performance of incentives, rewards, and promotional campaigns to evaluate effectiveness and commercial return.
Monitor ROI, promotional spend, and cost exposure, ensuring offers support key commercial KPIs.
Identify and mitigate abuse risks, bonus leakage, and other inefficiencies in promotional activity.
Support offer design and pricing strategies through commercial modelling and performance analysis.
Provide clear insights and recommendations to VIP and Promotions Managers to help plan, optimise, and monitor promotional strategies.
Work closely with the Data Analytics team to develop and improve tools that support performance monitoring and cost control.
Collaborate with the Finance team to understand how marketing KPIs translate into financial performance and identify opportunities to optimise promotional impact.
Produce regular performance reports and actionable insights to support decision-making.




Requirements:

Bachelor's Degree required (Marketing or Data Science an advantage).
2+ years' experience in a commercial role within the gaming industry.
Highly organised and proactive, with strong attention to detail.
Ability to work independently and meet tight deadlines.
Data-driven with strong commercial awareness.
Strong prioritisation skills and an enthusiastic can do attitude.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.

IT Analyst - Sales, Finance & Marketing

At Barilla, we believe that every great journey begins with passion and a bold vision. From our humble beginnings in 1877 in a small shop in Parma, Italy, we’ve blossomed into a global leader in the food industry, guided by our commitment to progress and excellence.

Today, Barilla is an international group present in more than 100 countries and we are proud to be recognized as the world’s top food company by reputation. description_full:
We believe it takes great people to create a great product. That’s why our team lives our company values, and we hire based on them, too. Since 2010, Pipedrive has been on a mission to support sales and marketing teams with easy-to-use, powerful tools that make everyday work faster and easier. Today, our cloud-based software is trusted by over 100,000 companies and used in 179 countries. We have grown from a five-person team to a truly international company of over 850+ people, representing more than 50 nationalities, with ten offices distributed across Europe and the US. In 2020, Pipedrive received a majority investment from Vista Equity Partners, a global investment firm that invests exclusively in enterprise software, data and technology-enabled businesses, making Pipedrive the fifth unicorn from Estonia.

We're looking for a SEM Analyst to join our Berlin or London office. In this role, you'll get a chance to be the analytical backbone of Pipedrive's SEM team - building the data foundation that powers faster, bolder paid search decisions at a global scale.You'll be joining a strong martech environment - our Marketing Analytics team has already built a solid, well-structured data foundation. Your job isn't to start from scratch; it's to extend and deepen what exists, bringing SEM-specific insight and channel expertise that takes our data capability to the next level.

If you're someone who enjoys digging into data, uncovering the story behind performance and turning complex datasets into clear actions, we'd love to meet you!

We welcome applications from data or marketing analysts with paid search exposure, as well as SEM specialists who are passionate about moving toward a more analytical and technical track.

Your new adventure:

Reporting & In-depth Analysis (70%):


Build and maintain SEM performance dashboards on top of our existing data infrastructure - clear, reliable and built for both at-a-glance reporting and deep troubleshooting
Develop deep expertise in the Google Ads API to unlock granular data at keyword, search term, bid strategy and audience levels - building the analytical layer that takes our SEM insight from good to exceptional
Go beyond platform-native reporting - joining ad platform data with internal metrics to connect paid search activity to real downstream business outcomes
Design and track A/B tests - from hypothesis through to conclusion - ensuring learnings are clearly documented and translated into the team's next move
Turn analytical findings into sharp, actionable recommendations that the team can act on with confidence


Campaign Management (30%):


Support the creation, management and optimisation of paid search campaigns across Google Ads and Microsoft Ads
Conduct keyword research, ad copy testing, bid management and negative keyword development
Monitor campaign performance and flag issues and opportunities proactively


Does this sound like you?


3+ years of experience in a data/marketing analytics or SEM role with a strong focus on reporting and analysis
Proficiency in Tableau/Looker/BI tools for data visualization and dashboarding
Practical experience using SQL and Python (Pandas/NumPy) for data manipulation and handling large datasets.
Comfortable working with multiple data sources - platform exports, CRM data and internal data tables
Familiarity with Google Ads data - you understand what the metrics mean in a paid search context
An analytical mindset, you ask why before you answer what
A background in maths, computer science, economics or a related field is a plus
Familiarity with the Google Ads API is a plus
Experience/interest in utilising AI for data automation/analysis is a plus


Why Pipedrive?


People-first culture - Be part of a team that values authenticity, champions collaboration, and supports each other—no egos, just teamwork. Work alongside top talent from around the world in an inclusive space where different perspectives fuel our best ideas. Everyone is welcome

description_full:
Help Us Change the Way the World Works

Build something that matters.

Langdock exists to change the way the world works, bridging the gap between what technology can do and what people actually do with it. We bring all leading AI models into one secure, model-agnostic platform and make them usable across entire organizations. Over 6,000 companies use our platform every day, from fast-growing startups to some of Europe's largest enterprises. Their employees open Langdock to draft strategies, analyze documents, or automate workflows - helping them to work smarter, think more creatively, and reach their full potential.

What "Marketing Operations" Means Here

Every function at Langdock runs with far fewer people than a company of our size normally would. That is only possible because we use AI aggressively to multiply the impact of small teams. Marketing is no exception.

But using AI is not the same as building with it. This role is for someone who builds the systems that make the whole team faster: skills, automations, pipelines, and custom interfaces. You are not the person who occasionally writes a prompt. You are the person who decides what gets built, makes it work reliably, and makes it accessible to colleagues who are not technical.

You will also be Langdock's internal proof point. When we tell customers that AI can transform how their teams work, you are part of the evidence. The work you ship internally becomes the stories we tell externally, and sometimes the product features we build next.

What You Will Actually Do


Build and maintain marketing skills on the Langdock platform. Brand voice assistants, research tools, brief generators, campaign reviewers. You build them, keep them working as the platform evolves, and make sure the team actually uses them.
Keep our documentation AI-ready. We store our knowledge in GitHub. You make sure it stays structured, up to date, and written in a way that AI agents can actually use.
Automate content workflows. From the first draft to the published output, there are repetitive steps in every content process. You find them, automate them, and make sure the quality holds. You are not replacing judgment with automation; you are freeing up space for judgment.
Maintain our AI-generated slide workflow. We produce a lot of decks: sales decks, workshop material, and customer presentations. You own the pipeline that generates them, keep the templates aligned with the design system, and make sure the output is good enough that people actually use it instead of starting from scratch.
Build custom interfaces when skills are not enough. Sometimes the problem needs a proper tool. You build simple internal dashboards, workflows, and interfaces when that is what the situation calls for.
Enable the rest of the team. You share what you build, document how it works, and help non-technical colleagues get more out of AI than they could on their own. You are not the only person who should be good at this.


What Makes This Different

Most "AI in marketing" roles involve using tools built by others. This is about building the tools the team depends on.

You will work directly on Langdock's own platform. When you hit something it cannot do, that feedback goes straight to the product team. When new features ship, you are the first person figuring out how to apply them. You are both a power user and a builder, and the things you learn in one role directly improve the other.

The patterns you develop internally also get productized. What you build for marketing becomes the template for what we recommend to customers.

You Might Be a Fit If...


You have built real things with AI. Not demos. Not one-off prompts. Workflows that run in production, tools that other people depend on, systems that improve over time.
You are technically capable of building what the problem requires. You can write code, connect APIs, and debug a misbehaving workflow at 2 am before a campaign goes out.
You do not just use AI tool

description_full:
AI Agents to Bridge the Gap between Engineering & Purchasing to reduce manufacturing waste




My co-founder Ian and I combine over 15 years of industry experience, working with companies like SEW-Eurodrive, Terex, Bosch and Carl Zeiss. We co-founded Resourcly, a Project A-backed, industrial tech start-up to help industrial manufacturers streamline complex portfolios to improve EBIT while preventing and reducing idle inventory from being scrapped.

With our solution, we want to solve industry problems that we have tried to address for the last decade in the manufacturing companies we worked for, but that have so far been out of reach.

With new technology and AI, that can finally happen.

Complex processes and products, lack of engineering talent and massive labour costs as well as a lack of intelligent automation have remained the industry’s most pressing challenge. With LLMs and agents, we finally have the tools to solve this.

Our vision is that future purchasing and engineering processes will be orchestrated and run by an AI workforce - ensuring reliable and cost-efficient procurement and flow of goods in an uncertain and complicated world with finite resources.




🦾 We're already live with global manufacturing leaders in Europe

💰 Backed by one of Europe's top early-stage funds (Project A)

📈 Growing fast and hiring exceptional talent to join early




Our goal is to build a small, but excellent team, therefore we are looking for a Product Marketing Manager. If you are excited to work in a very early-stage environment alongside the founding team, our Commercial Director, and support in building our Commercial function from the ground up - please reach out.







The Role

As our Product Marketing Manager, you will serve as the strategic bridge between product, revenue, and market intelligence at Resourcly. This is not a traditional marketing role — it is a revenue-growth function. You will focus on unlocking expansion opportunities from our existing product portfolio, identifying the product gaps that prevent us from reaching more profitable customer segments, and feeding our funnel with precision — not volume.

You will work closely with our CEO and Commercial Director, alongside Customer Success and the Founders, to turn market insights into pipeline and product capabilities into compelling narratives that win deals. As we scale, you will play a central role in building Resourcly's go-to-market foundation — from B2B SEO and GEO strategy to account-based marketing, sales enablement, and technical content that earns the trust of industrial buyers.




🪄 Key Responsibilities

Drive revenue growth from Resourcly's existing product portfolio by mapping upsell and cross-sell opportunities, identifying underused features, and working closely with Customer Success to convert product adoption into expansion conversations
Build and execute precision demand generation programs targeting Resourcly's known ICP — mid-to-large industrial manufacturers — through account-based marketing, tailored content, and high-intent outreach rather than volume-based tactics
Own Resourcly's B2B SEO and GEO strategy, ensuring we rank for high-intent queries and are cited by AI-powered search tools when industrial buyers search for inventory and procurement solutions
Conduct ongoing product gap analysis by synthesizing win/loss data, churn signals, ICP interviews, and competitive intelligence into structured briefs that inform both GTM positioning and roadmap decisions
Develop high-impact content and sales enablement assets — technical whitepapers, ROI calculators, case studies, and battle cards — while leading go-to-market execution for every new feature and module Resourcly ships
Serve as the voice of the market internally, translating buyer hesitations and customer language into actionable product requirements — and maintaining a quarterly product-market fit radar across NPS signals, ICP feedback, and competitor moves

description_full:
Du willst nicht einfach nur Social-Media-Posts planen? Du willst eine ganze Berufsgruppe sichtbar machen, eine Community wachsen lassen und mit deiner Arbeit echten Impact haben? Dann lies weiter.




📍 Essen / Hybrid (min. 3 Tage Office, 2 Tage Remote)

🕒 Vollzeit

🚀 Start: zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt




Wer wir sind

The PA Company ist der zentrale Wegbegleiter für Physician Assistants (PAs) in Deutschland – von der Hochschulwahl über Fortbildungen bis zum Traumjob. Mit unserer PA App, einem wachsenden Blog, Podcast, Newsletter und starken Social-Media-Kanälen (Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, Facebook, YouTube, WhatsApp) erreichen wir rund 80 % aller PAs in Deutschland.

Wir sind ein kleines, schnelles Team mit großem Anspruch. Gegründet von Tim und Hendrik, arbeiten wir täglich daran, das Berufsbild PA in Deutschland nach vorne zu bringen. Jetzt brauchen wir dich, um unser Marketing auf das nächste Level zu heben.




Was sind Physician Assistants?

Physician Assistants (PAs) sind hochqualifizierte medizinische Fachkräfte mit Hochschulabschluss, die Ärztinnen und Ärzte im Behandlungsalltag unterstützen und delegierbare medizinische Aufgaben übernehmen. Sie sind ein zentraler Bestandteil moderner Versorgungskonzepte und tragen dazu bei, dem Fachkräftemangel im Gesundheitswesen entgegenzuwirken. Sie arbeiten in Kliniken, Praxen und MVZs, ein junges, wachsendes Berufsbild mit stetig steigenden Studierendenzahlen.




Deine Rolle

Du übernimmst das gesamte operative Marketing von den Gründern – und machst es besser, als wir es je konnten. Du bist kein Zuarbeiter, sondern die Person, die Marketing bei uns eigenständig verantwortet und weiterentwickelt. Dein Ziel: Unsere Community und Reichweite in echte App-Nutzer:innen verwandeln.




Was dich erwartet

▸ Social Media – Du steuerst unsere Social-Media-Kanäle (Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, Facebook, YouTube) eigenständig – von der Content-Planung über die Erstellung (Text, Grafik, Video) bis zum Community Management. Hendrik steht dir als Gesicht vor der Kamera zur Verfügung.

▸ App-Kommunikation – Du verantwortest die gesamte Kommunikation rund um die PA App: E-Mail-Kampagnen, Push-Benachrichtigungen, Ankündigungen zu Fortbildungen, Accelerator-Terminen und neuen Features.

▸ Content & Newsletter – Du entwickelst und setzt einen Content-Plan um, schreibst unseren Newsletter über Mailchimp und produzierst Inhalte, die unsere Zielgruppe wirklich bewegen.

▸ Community – Du pflegst und baust unsere bestehende Community (Social Media, Newsletter, App) aus – sie soll mit dem wachsenden Berufsbild mitwachsen.

▸ B2B Paid Ads – Du setzt erste Google-Ads-Kampagnen auf, mit Fokus auf Kliniken, Praxen und MVZs, die PAs suchen.

▸ Events, Partnerschaften & PR – Bei Bedarf unterstützt du bei Events, Kooperationen und Pressearbeit.




Dein Ziel in 12 Monaten

Die Nutzerzahlen unserer PA App signifikant steigern und unsere Community-Reichweite mit dem wachsenden Berufsbild mitwachsen lassen. Daran ist auch ein leistungsbasierter Bonus gekoppelt – 10 % deines Jahresbruttos bei Zielerreichung.




Was du mitbringst

Must-Have:

▸ Mindestens 2–3 Jahre praktische Erfahrung im Marketing – uns ist egal, was auf deinem Abschlusszeugnis steht. Was zählt, ist was du kannst.

▸ Du hast Social-Media-Kanäle eigenständig betreut und organisch wachsen lassen.

▸ Du erstellst Content selbst: Texte, Grafiken (Canva), Kurzvideos (CapCut o. ä.) – du brauchst kein Team, um Ergebnisse zu liefern.

▸ Du hast Grundkenntnisse in Google Ads und Erfahrung mit E-Mail-Marketing (idealerweise Mailchimp).

▸ Du nutzt KI-Tools wie ChatGPT, Claude oder Midjourney nicht aus Neugier, sondern als festen Bestandteil deines Arbeitsalltags. Du siehst KI als Verstärker deiner Arbeit, probierst ständig neue Tools aus und hast Spaß daran, noch effizienter zu werden.

description_full:
Wir suchen dich 



Wir sind ein erfolgreiches, inhabergeführtes und eigenfinanziertes SaaS-Unternehmen mit einem schönen Campus im Düsseldorfer Medienhafen. Seit 2004 bauen wir Telefonielösungen, inzwischen mit knapp 300 Kolleg:innen und arbeiten dabei crossfunktional, selbstorganisiert, immer fair und auf Augenhöhe.






Das machen wir und darum brauchen wir dich



Unser Hauptprodukt ist sipgate – die Telefonanlage in der Cloud mit Apps, CRM-Integrationen und über 150 weiteren Business Funktionen. Mittlerweile nutzen über 35.000 B2B-Kund:innen sipgate und es laufen mehr als 10 Millionen Gespräche pro Monat über unsere digitalen Fest- und Mobilfunknetze. Die Weiterentwicklung unserer Produkte stoppt natürlich nie und in unseren Köpfen schwirren zahlreiche konkrete Ideen herum, sei es im Bereich der KI-Features oder Integrationen, die wir möglichst rasch umsetzen möchten. Und dafür brauchen wir dich – mit dir gemeinsam wollen wir unsere sipgate-Produktvisionen für unsere Kund:innen erlebbar machen.






So sieht dein Arbeitsalltag bei sipgate aus:


Das Ergebnis verantworten: Du verstehst, was ein Product Outcome ist, und möchtest diesen aktiv verantworten. Dein Ziel ist es, Mehrwert für unsere Kunden und unser Unternehmen zu schaffen.
Discovery: Du kannst Unternehmensstrategie und Geschäftsziele in Opportunities (Canvases) überführen. Dabei stellst du sicher, dass unsere Produkte nicht nur gut, sondern großartig werden.
Roadmap planen: Du übersetzt Opportunities in ein realistisches Backlog und sorgst für eine klare Priorisierung, damit dein Team immer weiß, worauf es ankommt.
Get it done: Du bringst Produkte und Features auf die Straße. Du arbeitest eng mit deinem Team zusammen und sorgst dafür, dass aus Ideen echte Erfolge werden.
Lernen & Wachsen: Du bringst Skills, Ideen oder Tools mit, die uns alle weiterbringen, und bist immer bereit, selbst dazuzulernen
… wenn du Lust auf einen kleinen Einblick in die Arbeit eines POs bei sipgate werfen möchtest, schau gern hier in den Talk von Antina und Carina rein :)





Das bringst du mit:


Du bringst mehrjährige Erfahrung als Business Product Owner, Produktmanager oder in vergleichbaren Positionen im SaaS-Umfeld mit und hast Lust 2-3 Mal die Woche an unseren Campus in Düsseldorf zu kommen. 
Du übernimmst gerne Verantwortung und fühlst dich in agilen Arbeitsweisen wohl – für dich sind dies nicht nur Schlagworte, sondern aktive Prinzipien.
Du bist nicht nur ein Teamplayer, sondern hast auch ein gutes unternehmerisches Gespür. Du erkennst Chancen und gestaltest Prozesse mit einem klaren unternehmerischen Fokus.
Ungewisse Situationen schrecken dich nicht ab, sondern motivieren dich. Du siehst Herausforderungen als Gelegenheit, Neues zu entdecken und voranzutreiben.
Ehrliches Feedback, offene Kommunikation und kurze Entscheidungswege sind für dich das A und O.
...wenn du das Gefühl hast, du bist der oder die Richtige für uns, auch wenn du nicht bei jedem Punkt der Jobbeschreibung ein Häkchen für dich setzen kannst, komm gerne auf uns zu. Lass uns quatschen und herausfinden, ob wir trotzdem zueinander passen!





So tickt sipgate


Wir sind von Kopf bis Fuß agil und das nicht nur auf dem Papier. Wie wir arbeiten, siehst du hier.
Wir haben flache Hierarchien und Peer-Verantwortung ist unser Credo – das bedeutet: Verantwortung übernehmen, eigene Entscheidungen treffen und aufeinander acht geben stehen hoch im Kurs.
Wir haben ein transparentes Gehaltsmodell und keine individuellen Gehaltsverhandlungen. Wie genau das funktioniert erfährst du hier.
Nachhaltigkeit ist für uns wichtig – unser Fuhrpark besteht nur aus E-Autos, private Autos laden während der Arbeitszeit kostenlos, wir beziehen unseren Strom von der Solaranlage, wir essen bio und regional und versuchen Verschwendung zu vermeiden.

tl;dr

After 14 years in construction tech, Lea & Jonas are building the intelligence layer for the construction industry
The largest construction enterprises in Europe — with a combined project volume of over €30bn annually — rely on Kuro every day to automate workflows and make faster, better decisions
You will be one of Kuro's first Customer Success hires - working directly with enterprise customers to drive adoption and turn early wins into long-term partnerships. No playbook exists yet. You'll build it.




About Kuro

Lea and Jonas scaled two of Europe's leading construction tech companies - Capmo and alcemy - and saw firsthand the challenges holding the industry back. Now they're tackling the root cause: the 50% of construction work still lost to manual data handling.

Kuro is the the intelligent project data system for construction. We're reimagining how construction professionals get work done and make decisions by leveraging AI to augment back-office workflows and make core data accessible.

After one year on the market leading construction enterprises with a combined construction volume >30 billion annually use Kuro everyday to automate workflows and make faster, better decisions.

We are 10 people based in Berlin. You will join a group of ex-technical founders and builders that are ambitious and kind.

Learn more: Join Kuro - Berlin




About the role




The Opportunity

As a (Junior) Customer Success Manager at Kuro you will:

Join Kuro as one of our early team members, where your input will significantly influence our product, processes, and culture. Your contributions will lay the groundwork for Kuro's future, balancing rapid iteration, customer value delivery, and scalability planning.
Help build the foundations of customer success at Kuro — improving workflows, preparing customer-facing systems, and ensuring our tools and processes are ready for scale.
Serve as a key point of contact for many active users, helping them integrate Kuro into their daily workflows.
Grow into greater ownership within customer success as the company and customer base expand.




Role and responsibilities

Customer Onboarding & Support
Support onboarding of new customers and guide users through the first steps with Kuro.
Monitor incoming support requests across tickets and live chat, ensuring timely responses and resolution of customer issues.
Customer Success Operations
Create onboarding materials and maintain our knowledge base, especially when new features are released, to support scalable customer self-service.
Help utilizing AI to improve and automate our internal processes and insights generation.
Drive Adoption & Engagement
Monitor usage to understand usage patterns across different user groups.
Derive insights and ideas on how to further foster adoption of Kuro to help our users get the most value out of our software.
Customer Voice to Product
Gather feedback from users and communicate insights to the product team.
Help identify common challenges or feature requests from customers.




Your profile

You are fluent in German and English.
You have up to 2 years of experience in customer success, operations, consulting, or a similar role (internships or early-stage startup experience are also valuable).
Experience in the construction industry is a plus.
You enjoy improving processes, testing tools, and figuring out how things can work better.
You enjoy helping customers solve problems and communicating clearly with different stakeholders.
You show a high work ethic, are hands-on, and move things forward with energy and ownership.
You are curious about AI, SaaS products, and the digitization of traditional industries such as construction.
You are eager to learn, take on responsibility over time, and grow with the company as Kuro scales.

description_full:
Our partners from Nike Germany are looking for an Energy Marketing Specialist as a maternity leave cover in their Energy Team, located in Berlin, with a limited contract.




BECOME A PART OF THE TEAM! Nike Energy is an exceptional team within Nike that creates authentic connections with our most discerning consumers, helping them fall in love with new ideas from Nike and drive the future of Sport & Culture.




WHO ARE WE LOOKING FOR?

We are looking for a marketing talent with a deep understanding of progressive youth culture in Germany & connections to visionary creatives from various creative fields, especially in Berlin. In depth knowledge of the fashion and or art industry & network is preferred. 




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

As the Energy Marketing Specialist Berlin, Germany you will be a part of the Nike Central Marketing Team, reporting directly to the Energy Marketing Director, Central. Based in Berlin, this role will be responsible for balancing relationship management & marketing execution through our key brand ambassador relationships across the Nike ecosystem, focused on Berlin and Germany. 

Specifically, you will be responsible for:

Identifying, managing and maintaining a network of cultural ambassadors in Berlin & Germany to cultivate the brand and create the future of sport
Providing exceptional service and brand management to all brand ambassador relationships
Consistently engaging in Berlin’s rich cultural landscape, identifying key consumer insights and driving an insight loop back into Geo & Global, including Brand and Product Teams
Feeding key brand ambassador relationships strategically into Geo & Global campaign work to catalyze our Brand and advance our position within our key marketing fields (Sportswear, Running, Football, Fitness)
Support driving key “energy” campaigns around our most limited & collaboration products with our most visionary led marketplace partners
Support creating marketing concepts and stories aimed at identified brand ambassadors pushing the boundaries of creativity and sport and advancing the notion of sports culture




WHO WILL YOU WORK WITH?

In this role you will work fluidly across Nikes Marketing Team, to drive better relationships with your consumer & partners in the city/country. You will also work cross-functionally in the Berlin office with key partners such as Brand Marketing, Comms and Marketplace.

The shape of your day-to-day will vary significantly, and your style and influence will have to adapt accordingly. You therefore must be collaborative, intuitive, and maximize the effectiveness of individual as well as collective thinking. You must multiply the opinions and expertise of those you engage with to apply coherent strategy and thinking that leads to new opportunities to continue to apply new thinking.




WHAT WE ASK OF YOU

In-depth understanding of youth culture, music as well as fashion, art and design. Strong understanding & network in the fashion industry is a plus.
A minimum of 3 years experience in brand and/or brand functional management, with a good understanding of cross- functional marketing.
Ability to build and maintain strong relationships with external partners in these fields
Demonstrated experience in cultural marketing, including developing and executing marketing plans, identifying seeding strategies, creating content briefs, and supporting the development of innovative marketing strategies.
Proven ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and external agencies to ensure timely execution of marketing plans.
Experience in prioritizing and managing multiple deliverables simultaneously, delivering results under tight and complex timelines.
Strong attention to detail and intrinsic passion for flawless execution.
Strong sense of ownership, proactivity, and accountability.
A strong team player who is open to grow themselves and others
